sourcePrompt: A CH-47 Chinook heavy-lift helicopter, about sixteen meters of fat straight fuselage with no tail rotor at all - its silhouette is the two tall rotor pylons, one at the nose and a taller one at the tail, carrying huge overlapping three-bladed rotors. Long sponsons run low along each side housing fuel and the four-wheel landing gear, and a loading ramp closes the rear. Parked level and heavy. Olive-drab, twin-rotor and unmistakable.
